purpose: BLIND CONFIRMATORY v4. Tests the post-v3 queue: (1) decode_dispatch bytes floor (tiny cells route to the dequant path), (2) split-K per-split work floor, (3) universal-constant revert, (4) group-size-keyed prefill config. v1-v3 verdicts stand.
frozen_commit: 80bda12 (grouped-nf4-gemm main). No code/config/harness/reducer changes between this stamp and reported results.
provenance_of_bars: All thresholds derive from pre-stamp data: the committed v4 exploratory runs (bench/phase2/sweeps/v4_prefill_{a5000,a2000}.json — the config-rule regret table; v4_floor_{a5000,a2000}.json — floor routing behavior), the v3 confirmatory, and the two decode sweeps. The v3 methodology law is applied: latency-bound cells carry PAIRED or loosely-banded criteria only; hard vs-dequant bars live on bandwidth-bound cells. Floor/split dev cells (Switch, granite-dn, Scout-dn, Hunyuan-dn) are development shapes (burned in v3) used to verify MECHANISM, not to claim novel wins; the prefill claim's census cells are long-standing development shapes — v4 makes PAIRED config claims on them, which the paired design keeps honest.

devices: FRESH RunPod SECURE A5000 (sm_86) provisioned AFTER this stamp
home RTX A2000 12GB (co-resident services; tiny-cell timings jitter up to ~1.75x on identical code — measured, disclosed, bars account for it)
runs_per_rep: decode: 8 census + bench/phase1/heldout_v4_floor_cells.json (Switch gu/dn = below-floor, granite dn = just-above-floor, Scout dn + Hunyuan dn = split cells) x backends {dequant_grouped, fused_nf4, fused_routed}
prefill_s2048: 8 census x backends {dequant_grouped, fused_nf4, fused_nf4_v3prefill}
protocol.reps: n=3 fresh-process invocations of each run per device; property suite (44 tests) once per device first.
protocol.reduction: bench/phase1/reduce_confirmatory_v4.py reading frozen_verdict_criteria_params from THIS file. NOT-RUN exclusion as always.
protocol.report_only: Universal-constant revert (v3 established noise-equivalence on the 26-SM card) and split-K paired gains (v3 confirmed) are REPORTED, not re-judged. Prefill vs-dequant on gate_up cells is reported at full volume (known below parity; the mainloop rewrite is future work).

frozen_verdict_criteria.F1_no_catastrophe: fused_routed median vs dequant >= 0.55 on EVERY decode cell, both devices. The v3 Switch class measured 0.24-0.35; same-code jitter on the A2000 measured as low as 0.57 on a single timing pair, so 0.55 at 3-rep median separates catastrophe from jitter.
frozen_verdict_criteria.F2_floor_identity_a5000: On the two below-floor Switch cells, routed median vs dequant in [0.75, 1.35] on the A5000 (routing means running the dequant path; the band is measurement noise). A2000 floor cells are REPORTED only (tiny-cell jitter law).
frozen_verdict_criteria.F3_eligible_identity: On every eligible (above-floor) decode cell, PAIRED routed vs fused median in [0.85, 1.18] both devices — dispatch must be a near-free wrapper where it chooses fused.
frozen_verdict_criteria.P1_prefill_config: PAIRED new-config vs retired-config median >= 1.15 on >= 6/8 prefill census cells per device. Exploratory measured 1.22-1.82 on 16/16.
frozen_verdict_criteria.P2_prefill_floor: fused vs dequant median >= 0.75 on >= 6/8 (A5000) / >= 4/8 (A2000) prefill cells — bars set from the exploratory's own distributions (A5000 7/8, A2000 4/8).
frozen_verdict_criteria.P2b_prefill_wins_a5000: fused vs dequant median >= 1.3 on >= 2/8 prefill cells on the A5000 (exploratory: down-projections at 1.88/1.94).
frozen_verdict_criteria.E1_census_energy: fused_routed J/token < dequant on 8/8 decode census cells per device (the replicated bandwidth-bound energy claim).
frozen_verdict_criteria.no_tune_clause: Failures reported at full volume; claims narrowed; no re-runs until green; no reduction changes after data exists.
